plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-581 RP18.P08.M09.S03-RP18.P09.M06.S06 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-581 implements descriptor-only coverage of the RP18 p08 closeout p09 foundation: the P08 Claude Review Packet tail (the twenty remaining rows), the full P08 Closeout And Next Handoff cycle, the full P09 Scope Inventory, Contract Draft, Type And Shape Definition, Primary Implementation Slice, Secondary Workflow Slice, and Permission And Audit Binding cycles, and the P09 Synthetic Fixture Set head (the first six rows) as nine descriptor-only sections over 150 planned rows with Hermes evidence (H18), Claude review packet (C18), and CP00-582 handoff, without opening AI legal workflows, precedent, clause markup, or DD extraction runtime or any other closed runtime surface.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-581 is a clean descriptor-only RP18 closeout pack (P08 closeout / P09 foundation) covering exactly 150 units RP18.P08.M09.S03-RP18.P09.M06.S06, risk class C. The pack-level production_ready claim is correctly false (runtime_ready false, implementation_layer descriptor); the "ai_legal_workflows_core_p08_closeout_p09_foundation_descriptor_verified" flag and the Hermes production_ready_candidate are gated on substantive coverage+slice validation, so there is no enterprise-trust overclaim. Independent grep of review-baseline.json confirms the deliverable distribution exactly (implementation 80, hermes_evidence 20, claude_review 20, test 9, security_audit 14, ui 7 = 150), matching the prompt, the CP581 registry requirements, the manifest delivery_counts, and the contract. All 7 required public exports exist and the validators perform real count/title/boundary checks (pack id, risk, unit count, first/last unit, duplicate ids, RP18-only, per-deliverable/phase/micro counts, per-row descriptor_only/no-runtime/customer-safe/no-raw-payload guards, attestation flags, authority boundary, handoff). model.test.js and the contract validator carry comprehensive CP581 assertions, and command-evidence.json records every required validation command (npm test, validate, closeout-pack[-plan]:validate, weighted/fullplan/spec/goal/rp00 validates, git diff --check) at exit_code 0. The no-write/no-real-data boundaries hold: the attestation chain never flips a safety flag to true, manifest guards (claude_not_final_approver, enterprise_trust_not_claimed_from_local_validation, no_hermes_runtime_receipt, no_state_transition_write, no-leak guards) are all true, and no_real_data is true. Review hardening is satisfied: the readiness auth preflight passed with valid non-empty JSON (ok:true, exit_code 0), the prompt excludes full diff/source with source_inspection_mode read_tools_used, summary-only review is prohibited and not used, request tools are limited to Read/Grep/Glob with forbidden_tools_present false, and forbidden_review_evidence enumerates failed_authentication_run, zero_byte_output, malformed_json_output, fenced_json_output, tool_call_shaped_output and missing-field/approval/trust blockers per CP178+. Claude is bound as a read-only independent reviewer and not final approver, with human final approval and adjudication/construction-inspection still pending downstream (status implemented_pending_review). Hermes patterns appear only as deterministic evidence-packet/receipt descriptors (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt false), not product runtime. Unrelated dirty files (UI-workstream progress-control-room and matter workbook docs) are correctly segregated under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved and not treated as pack scope. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-581 ai legal workflows core p08 closeout p09 foundation descriptor pack
